title: Iran fiscal system statistics, 1921-1979, extracted from Encyclopaedia Iranica's narrative article
source_org: Encyclopaedia Iranica Foundation (article), citing Bharier (1971), Karshenas (1990), Moghadam, Kaldor, Motamen as original sources
extraction_method: Manual extraction of every year-tagged statistic explicitly stated in the article's prose (not a table in the original -- Iranica articles are narrative). Each row preserves the article's own citation to its underlying academic source (mostly Bharier 1971 and Karshenas 1990, both with specific page/table numbers) so the claim can be traced further if the underlying book is ever obtained. No number was calculated, interpolated, or estimated -- every value is quoted or directly restated from the source text.
retrieved_by: claude (manual transcription from already-downloaded article text)
time_coverage: 1921-1979 (irregular: single years, multi-year periods, and decade-averages as stated in the source)
frequency: irregular -- whatever periodization the original article used
license_terms: Encyclopaedia Iranica -- academic reference, cite the article
notes: This is narrative-derived, not a primary statistical table -- treat as a secondary source one step removed from Bharier/Karshenas's original tables. Still valuable: it's the only source in this project giving fiscal structure (oil revenue share, military vs. education spending share, tax composition) continuously across BOTH Pahlavi reigns (1921-1979), not just the 1950s-60s window the primary World Bank extraction covers.
